summary:
The most accurate statement is that separation typically begins at the peripheral edge rather than presenting maternally at the vulva; blood collection can occur as part of normal processes post-separation. Thus, option B stands out as incorrect based on these considerations.

The final answer provided by Agent2 would be an explanation for why each other choice could potentially describe aspects related to placentophagy without directly addressing all specifics required here. Let me refine this summary focusing strictly on your requirements while ensuring clarity and relevance specifically tied back towards identifying which single fact among those listed doesn’t hold under standard medical understanding concerning placental detachment mechanisms associated primarily with labor progression dynamics involving maternal-fetal interface changes over time.

**Refined Summary:** Placental separation usually initiates near the outer margins instead of appearing externally via vaginal presentation alone; thus indicating how options describing internal physiological events better fit established knowledge whereas external visual cues might mislead unless clinically assessed properly throughout delivery stages. Hence, stating “maternal side...presents” inaccurately simplifies complex process making it least plausible amongst choices presented relative to actual obstetric practice guidelines surrounding term pregnancies & deliveries.
